whatWasImplemented: src/lib/chat/response-matcher.ts — pure matcher module. normalize() NFD-decomposes + strips combining marks + lowercases + collapses whitespace so diacritics ('café'->'cafe') and case ('HELP') match reliably. triggerMatches() uses an escaped, \W-anchored regex so word boundaries are enforced (VAL-RESP-009: 'help' no longer matches 'helper' / 'alphelp'). Multi-word triggers match as phrases ('business hours' vs 'businesshours'). matchKeywordRule() sorts by priority DESC, created_at ASC so higher-priority wins and ties go to older rules (VAL-RESP-010). classifyIntent() calls gpt-4o-mini with a jsonSchema-bound {intent: string} output and returns null on 'other' / empty. matchResponse() orchestrates keyword-first, then intent-only-if-site-has-intent-rules (VAL-RESP-011 gate).
Switched from zod to the AI SDK's built-in jsonSchema helper — zod isn't a project dep and the AI SDK's shape worked directly. The helper is a pass-through annotation, so tests stub it as identity.

src/app/api/chat/session/route.ts — wired matcher BEFORE query-rewrite + embed + RAG. Fetches rules with supabase.from('custom_responses').select(...).eq('site_id', X).eq('is_active', true).order(priority DESC).order(created_at ASC) — the DB index from M6F1 serves this directly. On a match, stores session with new cannedResponse field and returns sessionId; no OpenAI call (VAL-RESP-007: zero embed + zero generateObject).
src/lib/chat/session-store.ts — ChatSession gains optional cannedResponse. Present means the matcher fired and the stream route should emit it verbatim.

src/app/api/chat/stream/route.ts — early branch when session.cannedResponse is set: emits a plain text/plain stream of the canned text and persists the conversation via a shared helper (persistConversation) extracted from the existing onFinish. Normal RAG path unchanged.

src/__tests__/responses-matcher.test.ts — 26 unit tests covering normalize (lowercase, diacritics, whitespace), triggerMatches (case + word boundary + diacritic + multi-word + regex metachar escape), matchKeywordRule (empty rules, no match, single, priority winner, created_at tiebreak, intent-type ignored), matchIntentRule (exact label, miss, priority), classifyIntent (empty short-circuit, hit, 'other' null, empty label null), matchResponse (VAL-RESP-011 no classifier when no intent rules, keyword hit skips classifier, classifier only when no keyword hit).

src/__tests__/responses-integration.test.ts — 3 integration tests against /api/chat/session: (1) keyword match stores cannedResponse and never calls embed/generateObject/rewriteQuery (VAL-RESP-007); (2) keyword-only ruleset with no match NEVER invokes classifier and falls through to RAG (VAL-RESP-011 negative); (3) intent-rule present + keyword miss invokes classifier once and returns cannedResponse from intent match.

Test-suite fan-out: chat-session.test.ts / message-quota.test.ts / subscription-gate-routes.test.ts updated. The chat-session mock was rewritten with a table-routed fromBuilder so sites/profiles/custom_responses/... each have the right chain. The other two gained a custom_responses short-circuit case that returns empty, plus jsonSchema in their ai mocks (since response-matcher.ts imports it and the session route pulls matcher transitively).
whatWasLeftUndone: No per-session caching of classifier results across multiple messages in the same conversation — each POST to /api/chat/session is stateless and classifies once per call. Caching would benefit multi-turn conversations that repeatedly miss keyword rules, but it's not required by VAL-RESP-011 and adds storage complexity. Revisit if user-testing shows latency pain.

The matcher does not yet support exact phrase triggers with leading/trailing punctuation edge cases beyond \W boundaries (e.g. emoji-only messages). For Latin-script chat this is fine; internationalization would need locale-aware tokenization.
discoveredIssues: Three adjacent test files (chat-session, message-quota, subscription-gate-routes) all mock the 'ai' module minimally. Adding jsonSchema as a matcher import required extending each mock. Noted — any future 'ai' export added by a new matcher feature will hit the same spots. Option: a shared test helper that builds a permissive ai mock.

The session route's direct .from('custom_responses') chain length (select+eq+eq+order+order) is longer than prior Supabase mocks in the repo expected. The table-routed builder pattern in chat-session.test.ts is the cleanest fix; older tests use ad-hoc shape matching. Reusable helper would pay off but isn't urgent.

verification.testsRun: pnpm vitest run — 475 passed / 1 skipped / 61 files (+29 new: 26 matcher + 3 integration).

manualChecks.expected: null, generateObject not called (no intent rules)

filesChanged: src/app/api/chat/stream/route.ts (modified — canned-response early branch + persistConversation helper)
